perturbation.original_text: 5. All personal identifiable information will be treated as confidential and will not be disclosed to any other persons outside the requi rements of the above agreed purpose(s), without agreement of the ‘data controller’. Any organisational information marked as ‘commercial’ or ‘sensitive’ or by implication of the subject could prejudice the commercial interests of either party will be treat ed as confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. All personal identifiable information will be treated as confidential and will not be disclosed to any other persons outside the requirements of the above agreed purpose(s), unless explicitly stated by a senior manager of the contracting organisation. Any organisational information marked as ‘commercial’ or ‘sensitive’ or by implication of the subject could prejudice the commercial interests of either party may be disclosed, but only after notifying the LSP and obtaining written consent.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation adds an in-text contradiction by changing 'without agreement of the ‘data controller’' to 'unless explicitly stated by a senior manager of the contracting organisation' and changing 'will be treated as confidential' to 'may be disclosed, but only after notifying the LSP and obtaining written consent'. This means that the senior manager has the power to share the information at their discretion, introducing uncertainty, contradicting the primary principle of confidentiality set earlier in the document, particularly the previous section regarding legal action after data breaches, undermining data protection and privacy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any patient information, particularly their health or treatment details, is considered sensitive, but subject to the stipulations outlined in clauses 5 and 7. If such information was leaked to the wrong people, it could cause embarrassment to the patients concerned, their families and to the organisation. Legal action may be considered following such a breach of confidentiality based on the severity and scope of the leak. In addition any inappropriate or unauthorised disclosure of commercially sensitive information or unlicensed reuse of intellectual property could be subject to legal action.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that personal identifiable information would not be disclosed without agreement of the data controller. The changed text allows disclosure if explicitly stated by a senior manager, contradicting the original confidentiality clause. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which states that patient information is considered sensitive and that legal action may be considered following a breach of confidentiality. The change introduces a mechanism for authorized breaches, creating a direct contradiction and weakening the protections outlined in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Where the activities performed by the contractor (in this case LSP) do not require them to process information but they may become party to it by overse eing or overhearing, they will be required to keep such information confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Where the activities performed by the contractor (in this case LSP) do not require them to process information but they may become party to it by overseeing or overhearing, they will be required to keep such information confidential, unless such disclosure is necessary for the fulfillment of their contracted duties as assessed by the contractor.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by adding the clause 'unless such disclosure is necessary for the fulfillment of their contracted duties as assessed by the contractor.' This clause gives the LSP the power to determine what is necessary for the fulfillment of the contract and to disclose potentially confidential information if they deem it necessary. This can create ambiguity, as the LSP may have a different interpretation of 'necessary' than the DRC, and might contradict the previously stated obligation to keep the information confidential regardless, making enforcement challenging and creating a contradiction with the overall emphasis on confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Where the activities performed by the contractor (in this case LSP) do not require them to process information but they may become party to it by overseeing or overhearing, they will be required to keep such information confidential, unless such disclosure is necessary for the fulfillment of their contracted duties as assessed by the contractor.
perturbation.justification: The original text states a requirement to keep information confidential. The changed text adds an exception, allowing disclosure if 'necessary for the fulfillment of their contracted duties as assessed by the contractor.' The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. The change introduces an inconsistency because it now permits the contractor to disclose information that they were previously obligated to keep confidential. This creates a conflict within the clause itself because the LSP can now disclose potentially confidential information if they deem it necessary, making the initial obligation to keep such information confidential less enforceable and creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Any breach of the terms of this agreement may result in termination of arrangements ( including formal contracts) and legal action may be taken.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Any breach of the terms of this agreement will be reviewed by the DRC's legal team and may, depending on the severity and intent of the breach, result in termination of arrangements (including formal contracts) and legal action being taken.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation adds the phrase 'will be reviewed by the DRC's legal team and may, depending on the severity and intent of the breach,' to the original text. This phrase undermines the original, more definitive statement that any breach 'may result in termination...and legal action may be taken.' The insertion introduces subjectivity by specifying a review process and making potential actions contingent on severity and intent. This creates an in-text contradiction, as it is no longer certain that every breach will lead to termination or legal action and also creates ambiguity regarding the action that will be taken.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any breach of the terms of this agreement will be reviewed by the DRC's legal team and may, depending on the severity and intent of the breach, result in termination of arrangements (including formal contracts) and legal action being taken.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds subjectivity by specifying a review process and making potential actions contingent on severity and intent. This introduces an in-text contradiction, as it is no longer certain that every breach will lead to termination or legal action. Since the Changed Text and Contradicted Text are the same, and the Original text did not specify that there was a review process and the changed text introduces that review process with subjectivity, thus it creates a contradiction because the first clause is essentially saying any breach may lead to termination while the second says it will be reviewed and depending on its severity and intent of the breach termination might occur. Thus, a direct conflict exists.
